noveltyJustification: This pass identifies the exact two-coordinate-system handoff, reduces the six-row template-causality claim to four confirmed rows, and finds one topology/gold drift plus one unreconstructable provenance contradiction.
focus: Answer Q2 by tracing standalone packet-root paths, parent-hub-qualified paths, and their undefined serialization handoff across the six wrong-root rows.

graphEvents.label: four handoff one drift one provenance

label: four handoff one drift one provenance
Standalone packet routers and parent hubs use different valid roots without a serialization handoff contract.
Four wrong-root rows directly copy hub packet qualifiers onto packet-local leaf ids.
SD-020 gold flattens a physical shared asset into a nonexistent hub-root assets path.
SD-016 current gold and retained response agree on the packet-qualified path despite stored zero recall, leaving provenance unreconstructable.
Internal hub load addresses are hub-root-relative and packet-qualified; packet router leaf ids are packet-root-relative unless an explicit output namespace says otherwise.
Parent router resources stop at packet SKILL.md and do not cover second-layer leaf serialization.

direction: One universal wrong-root convention taught by the standalone smart-router template
reason: The standalone template consistently returns packet-local paths; the opposite convention comes from the parent-hub schema.

direction: All six wrong-root rows share one template cause
reason: Four are confirmed handoff collisions, SD-020 is topology-versus-gold drift, and SD-016 is inconsistent with the current fixture.
